description:
First implementation phase of bl-f021. Lands the nested-XDG layout (SPEC-clone-layout §3, post-bl-e9c2 revision) as the *only* layout `bl init` writes, and teaches `Store::discover` to read pre-XDG and bl-fc50 (hashed-XDG) clones so existing repos keep working unchanged. This is the safety net every later phase rests on — once dual-read ships, the migrate command (Phase 2) becomes a discrete, separately-shippable step.

This ball was originally scoped against bl-fc50's SPEC (hashed `<origin-key>`/`<path-hash>` keys, `state-repos/own/` checkouts, `active/`/`own/` view symlinks). SPEC-clone-layout was rewritten in bl-e9c2 to drop hashing entirely in favour of transparent percent-encoded paths and a nested per-clone tree, and to add a fourth config file (`clone.json`). This description has been re-planned against that rewrite. Three commits made under the prior scope were dropped (the f60262d vendored-SHA-256 + clone-identity commit, the 1bca052 hashed XDG path layer, and the 1b06aa7 three-file config types which need a fourth file added) and the slice restarts from main.

## Scope

Per SPEC-clone-layout §3 (layout), §4 (clone identity: `<enc-origin>`, `<enc-balls-tasks>`, `<nested-clone-path>`), §5 (bootstrap convention), §6 (four-file config: tracker.json + project.json + repo.json + clone.json with `clone.json ?? repo.json ?? project.json ?? built-in default` precedence), §7 (`Store::discover` materialization), §8 (worktree relocation), and §11 (the *read side* of migration — applying field renames in memory when reading old files, *without* committing the migration).

### Implements

1. **Transparent path materialization in `Store::discover`** (§7 steps 1–8).
   - Canonicalize + percent-encode the origin URL into `<enc-origin>` (§4). Frozen golden vectors per §14.2. RFC 3986 unreserved survives; `/`, `:`, `@`, `?`, `#`, `&`, space, and any non-ASCII byte become `%XX`. No hashing anywhere.
   - `<enc-balls-tasks>` = literal `balls%2Ftasks` (compile-time constant alongside the bootstrap branch name).
   - `<nested-clone-path>` = the clone's absolute git-dir path with leading `/` dropped, slashes preserved (§4).
   - `~/.local/state/balls/trackers/<enc-origin>/<enc-balls-tasks>/` checkout of the own branch.
   - On redirect, `~/.local/state/balls/trackers/<enc-state-url>/<enc-state-branch>/` checkout of the federated tracker. Single-hop check (§5, §14.5).
   - Per-clone tree: `~/.local/state/balls/{worktrees,claims,locks,plugins-auth}/<nested-clone-path>/`.
   - **No** `<origin-key>`/`<path-hash>` derivation, **no** `active/`/`own/` view symlinks, **no** `state-repos/` parent dir, **no** `~/.cache/balls/<origin-key>/` per-repo subtree. The shape is flat by §3.

2. **Four-file config readers** (§6.1, §6.2, §6.3, §6.4, §6.5).
   - `tracker.json` strict schema (only `state_url`/`state_branch`); §14.6.
   - `project.json` lenient-unknown reader; tracker-scope fields owned here.
   - `repo.json` lenient-unknown reader; tracker-scope fields appearing here abort the read (§14.9).
   - `clone.json` reader at `~/.config/balls/<nested-clone-path>/clone.json` — present only when set. Owns `stealth` + `tasks_dir`; may also carry any layered field as an override. Tracker-scope fields here also abort the read (§14.9).
   - `clone.json ?? repo.json ?? project.json ?? built-in default` precedence merge (§6.5); §14.8 three-way test.

3. **`bl init` writes XDG exclusively, nested layout.**
   - No `.balls/` at the clone root, no `.gitignore` entries, no `balls: initialize` commit on main.
   - Pushes a `balls/tasks` orphan branch with default `repo.json` + `project.json`, no `tracker.json` (solo case).
   - §14.1, §14.19 (no on-main writes in steady state).

4. **Stealth via `clone.json`** (§4.1, §14.15).
   - `bl init --stealth [--tasks-dir <path>]` writes `~/.config/balls/<nested-clone-path>/clone.json` with `stealth: true` + `tasks_dir`.
   - On `stealth: true`, `Store::discover` skips origin/`trackers/`/redirect machinery entirely; reads tasks directly from `tasks_dir`.
   - Retires the previous revision's "synthetic origin-key" fallback — there is no special-case in the layout, only the read-time short-circuit.

5. **Dual-read for legacy layouts** (safety net).
   - **Pre-XDG layout detection** — `.balls/config.json` on main + `.balls/state-repo/` runtime + `.balls-worktrees/` in tree. When present, read from the in-repo layout instead of XDG; warn one line `legacy layout in use; run \`bl migrate\` to relocate` per §12.
   - **bl-fc50 hashed-XDG layout detection** — `~/.local/state/balls/<origin-key>/state-repos/own/.balls/` exists (the bl-fc50 own checkout). When found, read under that layout and warn similarly. The field-name renames (`delivery`→`integrate`, `review.pre_check`→`review.gate_command`) and the `target_branch` drop are applied **in memory** on the way out of the reader, not written back.
   - **bl-ed32 two-file layout detection** — `state-repos/own/.balls/workspace.json` present. Same approach: read, apply renames in memory, warn.
   - Preference rule: prefer new (nested) over bl-fc50 (hashed) over bl-ed32 over pre-XDG. The migrate command in Phase 2 may run mid-way and leave the old around briefly; Phase 1 must already favour the new.

6. **Worktree relocation for new claims** (§8, §14.13).
   - `bl claim` invokes `git worktree add` against `~/.local/state/balls/worktrees/<nested-clone-path>/<bl-id>/`. On a legacy-detected clone, the legacy path is preserved (until Phase 2 migrate moves them).

### Explicitly NOT in scope

- `bl migrate` command itself — Phase 2 (bl-717e). Phase 2 covers both pre-XDG → nested and hashed-XDG → nested transitions (§11.1, §11.2).
- `bl prime --migrate` / auto-detect warning beyond the one-line legacy warning — Phase 3.
- `min_bl_version` bump on the orphan tracker — Phase 4.
- Cross-doc rename of SPEC-forge-gated-delivery §5 (the `delivery`/`target_branch` references) — lands with Phase 2.
- `bl doctor` enrichment for moved-clone detection (§14.14) — defer to a follow-up; Phase 1 just needs to not regress doctor's current behaviour.

## Conformance tests gated by this ball

SPEC-clone-layout §14 tests: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15, 20, plus §14.16/§14.17 *under the Phase-1 dual-read branch* (read old field names, warn, apply rename in memory). §14.19 (no on-main writes) gated on freshly-initialized clones; existing clones with legacy `.balls/config.json` on main don't satisfy this until Phase 2 runs.

Each test must exist and fail for the right reason against pre-implementation code before the ball lands. New-side assertions per SPEC-tracker-state §16.13 — no pinned old-binary fixtures.

## Why this ball, not three smaller ones

The path materialization, four-file readers, dual-read, and stealth handoff all bind into the same `Store::discover` function. Splitting them produces intermediate states where (a) discover knows the new paths but no file reader understands the new schema, or (b) the schema readers exist but no path code calls them, or (c) stealth works in clone.json reads but not in `Store::discover`'s short-circuit. All of those intermediate states require dead code on main and synthetic test fixtures that don't reflect any real lifecycle. Bundling lets the ball ship a coherent slice — "new clones use nested XDG, old clones still work, stealth has a real home."

## Risks

- `Store::discover` is hot — every `bl` invocation runs it. Phase 1's branch-on-layout logic must be O(1) on the common path; legacy-detection should short-circuit via a single stat (`clone.json` first, then `trackers/<enc-origin>/<enc-balls-tasks>/`).
- Percent-encoding must be **exactly reversible**: §10 requires `jq -Rr @uri` to produce the same bytes the binary does. The conformance vector set (§14.2) is the gate against drift; pick one well-known impl and freeze its output for the URL test corpus.
- Nested clone paths inherit the FS-permission and length limits of the host. A clone at `/home/<long-username>/<deeply/nested/project/path>` could push close to `PATH_MAX` once the worktree id and bl directories are appended. Document the limit; don't engineer around it (§13: no hashing for "compactness").
- Two clones of the same origin on the same machine share `trackers/<enc-origin>/<enc-balls-tasks>/` and may race fetches; git's own `index.lock` serializes. Conformance test §14.3 asserts the per-clone isolation under contention.
title: Phase 1: dual-read XDG layout, bl init writes XDG only
